title: str: unicode_ascii_iter_next advances a shared str iterator's it_index non-atomically (Objects/unicodeobject.c:14983 write vs unicodeiter_len:14997 read) -- this IS cpython#153928 (the str form; TSAN-0037 is the bytes analog)
description: The str iterator (unicodeiterobject / unicode_ascii_iter_next in Objects/unicodeobject.c) keeps its cursor in a plain struct field it->it_index and its source in it->it_seq, with no synchronization. When a single str iterator is shared across threads, unicode_ascii_iter_next reads+writes it->it_index (:14983) with no atomicity while unicodeiter_len (the __length_hint__ slot, :14997) plainly reads the same it->it_index -> data race on the cursor; and on exhaustion two threads can both run the it_seq = NULL / Py_DECREF(seq) path -> double-DECREF / use-after-free. This is the str form of the exact defect johng filed as cpython#153928 (which we reproduced and commented on); TSAN-0037 is the byte-for-byte-identical bytes-iterator (striter_next) form. Surfaced by fusil's --tsan shared-iterator op (PR #211/#213): op h shares one iter('A'*4096) by reference across workers, some advancing it via next() (writer) and some reading its cursor via operator.length_hint (unicodeiter_len).
sites: Objects/unicodeobject.c unicode_ascii_iter_next:14983 (WRITE it->it_index: the non-atomic cursor advance the fleet's next() drives)
Objects/unicodeobject.c unicodeiter_len:14997 (READ it->it_index: `len = PyUnicode_GET_LENGTH(seq) - it->it_index` for __length_hint__, driven by the fleet's length_hint reader)

Objects/unicodeobject.c unicode_ascii_iter_next exhaustion path (it->it_seq = NULL; Py_DECREF(seq) -- two threads both reach it -> double-DECREF, by inspection; same as the confirmed bytes analog TSAN-0037 / #153928)
race_kind: shared-iterator state: non-atomic cursor read/write (it_index) on a single shared str iterator -> data race (and OOB read past the string), plus an unsynchronized it_seq = NULL / Py_DECREF exhaustion path -> double-DECREF / use-after-free. A builtin sequence iterator's per-iteration state mutated with no per-object critical section (Yhg1s shared-builtin ruling).
shared_state: unicodeiterobject fields on a single shared str iterator: it_index (Py_ssize_t cursor) and it_seq (the source str)
severity: moderate-to-high (memory-unsafe: the it_index race is an OOB read, the it_seq double-DECREF is a refcount underflow / UAF). Sharing one iterator across threads is unusual in practice (lower real-world priority), but str declares no per-object locking. Already filed upstream (johng, #153928) and reproduced by us.
repro_reliability: The str race in #153928 was previously reproduced by us (non-atomic it_index read/write + the it_seq double-DECREF UAF via ASan) when analysing that issue. Fleet-08 confirms both the self-race (unicode_ascii_iter_next x2, 15 veh) and the length_hint face (| unicodeiter_len, 40 veh) = 55 vehicles across diverse modules. The packaged repro.py mirrors the fleet vehicle (8 threads, half advancing one shared iter('A'*4096) via next(), half reading its cursor via operator.length_hint); it targets the same it_index race but was not independently re-run in isolation this session.
suggested_fix: Same as the bytes analog / #153928: make it_index an atomic load/store (or take a per-iterator critical section over unicode_ascii_iter_next / unicodeiter_len), and release it_seq exactly once via an atomic exchange on exhaustion. The bytes/list/tuple/range iterators share this shape and want the same treatment.
related: cpython#153928 (johng) IS this bug -- the str iterator (unicode_ascii_iter_next) it_index race; we reproduced + commented on it. TSAN-0037 is the identical bytes-iterator (striter_next) form. TSAN-0039 is the struct.iter_unpack form (cpython#154013). The dict-iterator shared-cursor face folds to TSAN-0026.
notes: TSAN-0037's notes predicted this: 'the str iterator did NOT trip in fleet-06 (0 dirs) -- bytes dominated'. In fleet-08 (first fleet after Phase-4 A-E: roles + length_hint reader) the str iterator finally tripped (55 veh). Not a separate filing -- it IS #153928; cataloged so fleets dedupe it and to record the length_hint (unicodeiter_len) face. Candidate to add to the #153928 thread as confirmation that fusil now reproduces it directly.
